responses.text: Many women in Japan are employed in casual or non-permanent jobs, making them more vulnerable to economic shocks.
Suicide ranked first among causes of death among 30-34 year olds in Japan
Young people have been particularly affected by the pandemic.
The increase in mental trauma is one of the reasons for suicide.

The economic problems brought about by COVID-19 contributed to the increase in the number of suicides.
Japan's suicide rate fell 20% in April compared to the same period last year
The number of suicides among schoolchildren in Japan has increased significantly.
Males are more than twice as likely to commit suicide as females.
Suicide rates in Japan increased for the first time in 11 years during the pandemic.
Suicide rates have risen since March and peaked in October.
The number of suicides in Japan in 2020 was 20,919, which is 750 higher than in 2019.
The number of suicides among women has increased especially, by 83% over the same period, and by 22% compared with that of men.
Especially housewives are affected by the pandemic
The pandemic has increased anxiety levels in people who were left completely alone.
The family environment in Japan has changed due to the coronavirus epidemic.
Prime Minister of Japan Yoshihide Suga decided to introduce the post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation.
The phenomenon of hikikomori, or people who avoid contact and live as recluses, is present in Japan.

26% of women said they had faced employment problems such as layoffs since April, compared with 19% of men, according to a survey by the Japan Broadcasting Association.

The number of suicides in Japan decreased by 14% in the spring of 2020 compared to the average of the past four years.
A total of 440 primary and secondary school students in Japan committed suicide as of the end of November 2020.
The number of suicides under the age of 20 was 659, an increase of 10.0% year-on-year, the highest since 2000.
Suicide rates among women increased by 885 to 6,976 in 2020.

The COVID-19 epidemic has affected all aspects of Japanese society.
The post of Minister to combat loneliness and isolation was taken by Minister for Regional Development Tetsushi Sakamoto.
More than a third of households in Japan consist of one person.
Thousands of Palestinians demonstrated against the Abrahamic Agreement in the West Bank and Jerusalem.

New Taipei Civil Affairs Minister Ke Ching-chung said that in traditional urban areas with high population densities such as Xinbei Banqiao, Xinzhuang, Wugu, and Sanchong, most park facilities have reached the end of use.

Perseverance has an experimental installation MOXIE to receive oxygen from carbon dioxide in the atmosphere of Mars.

The Russian embassy in North Korea said it had released a Russian fishing vessel that had been detained 11 days earlier, saying it was leaving for a South Korean port where two South Korean crew members would come ashore.
The detention of the ship is political
North Korea detained a Russian fishing boat “Xiang Hai Lin 8” flying a South Korean flag and breaking into the territorial waters early on the 17th, with 15 Russians on board and 2 South Koreans.
The crew of the ship is being held in inhuman conditions.
North Korean border guards detained the Russian fishing vessel “Xianghailin-8” on the night of July 17
Diplomats are trying to find out why the crew of the ship is not allowed to leave the port
Japan has decided to withdraw from the International Whaling Commission (IWC) in 2019 to resume whaling from July.
Whale meat has been an important source of food for the Japanese since ancient times.
Last year, Japanese whale-hunting vessels caught at least 333 minke whales, or small fin whales, for what they described as "scientific research".
Consumption of whale meat has declined significantly in recent decades in Japan.
The Japan Fisheries Agency set a limit of 227 for whaling up to the end of December this year, including 52 minke whales, 150 Bryde whales and 25 Sei whales.
Japan will cut its whale hunt by two-thirds, but still insist on hunting about 330 whales a year
Ernest Hemingway's Farewell to Arms was specially translated by Najaf Daryabandari, who had the courage to translate the dialogue in a way that was not commonly used by translators at the time.
In the Soviet Union, it was difficult to find an apartment of an intelligent person in which there was no portrait of Hemingway
The novel "The Old Man and the Sea" is very simple in terms of story and language, but it has a deeper meaning.
Ernest Hemingway wrote from real, live experiences.
Najaf Daryabandari translated the works from English by maintaining the style of the author.
Hemingway and Tolstoy have written about love and war.
Ernest Miller Hemingway wants to show the world how small true needs are
Ernest Miller Hemingway discussed the effects of loneliness on people.
Hemingway's fame was exorbitant
Ernest Hemingway is one of the emblems of the modern world.
For weak souls, loneliness is torture.

The fully superconducting tokamak nuclear fusion experimental device (EAST) of the Hefei Institute of Materials of the Chinese Academy of Sciences has successfully achieved repeatable 120 million degrees centigrade and 101 seconds of plasma operation.
The International Thermonuclear Fusion Experimental Reactor (ITER) is humanity’s largest nuclear fusion project.
China has made significant progress in nuclear fusion research, transitioning from “following” to “leading” in some areas
The new stage of the ITER project is the assembly and installation of a fusion reactor
Russia is taking part in the project.
China's artificial sun has set a new record in its quest for fusion energy.
An international experimental thermonuclear reactor ITER is being built in France
China says its nuclear program is for peaceful purposes.
The task of the ITER project is to demonstrate the scientific and technological feasibility of using thermonuclear energy on an industrial scale
This project is a collaboration between China, the European Union, India, Japan, South Korea, Russia and the United States.

China has reached another milestone in its quest to realize a fusion reactor, and one of its artificial suns has managed to maintain extreme temperatures several times higher than its previous record.
China is accelerating its own pace of development, with the goal of designing a fully fledged fusion reactor capable of generating electricity.
The heart of ITER is tokamak, a toroidal installation for magnetic plasma retention developed by Soviet and Russian scientists.
Physicists hope to obtain a source of unlimited energy
In 2019, the process of creating a Chinese experimental reactor for thermonuclear fusion EAST will be completed
MIT has expanded on the tokamak device to build the SPARC device
The ITER project is based on the concept of the Tokamak installation developed in Russia.
